---
title: "Student Performance Analysis"
description: "A Python project analysing the relationship between study time and academic performance."
author: "Serena Emedeke"
date: 2026-04-16
categories: [Python, Data Analysis]
image: /images/student-project.png
image-alt: "Line chart showing study hours versus student scores"
format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
execute:
  engine: jupyter
  echo: true
  eval: true
---

## Overview

This project analyses the relationship between study hours and student performance using Python. It demonstrates how data can be organised, visualised, and interpreted to identify trends.

## Objectives

- Organise data in a clear and structured format  
- Analyse the relationship between study time and performance  
- Visualise results using Python  
- Interpret findings and draw conclusions  

## Python Code

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px

df = pd.DataFrame({
    "StudyHours": [1, 2, 3, 4, 5],
    "Score": [50, 55, 65, 72, 85]
})

df

fig = px.line(
    df,
    x="StudyHours",
    y="Score",
    markers=True,
    title="Study Hours vs Score"
)

fig
```
## Limitations and Next Steps

This analysis is based on a small dataset and is intended to demonstrate analytical techniques rather than draw real-world conclusions. Future work could involve using larger datasets, incorporating additional variables, and applying statistical methods to strengthen the analysis.


## Reflection

This project strengthened my ability to organise data, create visualisations, and interpret trends using Python.

It also demonstrated how data analysis can be used to communicate insights clearly, which is an important skill for future work in data analysis.
